summary.intent: Fix a series of bugs in the Gossamer VS Code extension (session tracking, DB schema, multi-repo support, server lifecycle) while adding new features including agent color mapping, markdown improvements, per-message author attribution, and UI polish to the session detail and list views.
summary.outcome: All requested bug fixes and features were implemented and compiled successfully: multi-repo DB client registry, schema push fix, checkpoint worktree relocation, per-message git author attribution via checkpoint commits, agent brand colors, post-render DOM search highlighting, copy-on-hover for messages, workspace-scoped session filtering, and live session detection from disk.

repo: pushSchema in server.ts splits schema.sql on ';\n' then filters chunks starting with '--', but every chunk in schema.sql starts with '-- CreateTable', so ALL 56 CREATE TABLE statements were silently discarded — fixed by stripping comment lines within chunks instead of filtering whole chunks
Entire.io hooks are configured in the agent's settings file (.claude/settings.json), not in .entire/settings.json; without hooks the CLI never writes to full.jsonl
Shadow branches (entire/<hash>-<id>) are written continuously during a session; entire/checkpoints/v1 only gets promoted at checkpoint boundaries, which can lag by hours or days

Each repo has its own SQLite DB keyed by path; the server previously used a single 'primary' db variable that broke cross-repo queries — replaced with a dbRegistry Map and helper functions allDbs()/dbForRepo()/resolveDb()

The gitlog npm package defaults to nameStatus: true, appending --name-status to git log; on large repos with 10,000 commits this exceeds Node's maxBuffer and throws silently, causing zero CheckpointIdGitOidJoin rows
macOS com.apple.provenance extended attribute can block SQLite I/O with 'disk I/O error'; fixed by adding retry logic that evicts the cached Prisma client, deletes the corrupt file, and retries
Author attribution should come from checkpoint commits (Checkpoint: <id> on entire/checkpoints/v1) not from git blame on the jsonl file; each checkpoint commit is authored by whoever ran Entire
git worktree add fails with 'branch already used by worktree' when two repos share the same branch name; fixed with --detach flag
The Entire CLI's full.jsonl lives at .entire/metadata/<sessionId>/full.jsonl on disk, updated live before any shadow branch commit; watching this file enables sub-3-second session appearance

code.finding: pushSchema was only called on the primary DB at startup; secondary repo DBs registered in config were never schema-pushed, causing 'table does not exist' errors for all indexers
Session sort was comparing cwd paths which break across machines; changed to compare basename(cwd) and repo remote URL for cross-machine same-repo grouping
findCommitForCheckpoint must pass nameStatus: false and execOptions: {maxBuffer: 100*1024*1024} to gitlog, otherwise large repos silently fail and return null
seenBlobShas check skips re-indexing if blob SHA unchanged; needed a bypass path for author backfill when gitUserName is NULL on existing rows
Context-based inline highlighting breaks markdown rendering on edge cases; switched to post-render DOM walking via TreeWalker API after React commits the tree
ClaudeTurnCard initialized toolsExpanded/thinkingExpanded to false unconditionally; auto-expand on search hit requires initializing from matchesAny()/toolHasMatch() helpers
spawnServer() guard only checked in-memory GossamerPanel.server; orphaned processes from VS Code crashes held the port and caused EADDRINUSE on next activation
ShadowSession and LogEvent lacked gitUserName/gitUserEmail columns; old DBs need ALTER TABLE migration in pushSchema (tolerant, swallows duplicate column errors)

workflow: When SQLite DBs are 4096 bytes (header only, no tables), pushSchema ran but silently executed zero statements — always verify table existence after schema push rather than trusting the 'schema ready' log line
For multi-machine repos, comparing git remote URLs is more reliable than comparing cwd paths for session ownership
git worktrees owned by a bare repo work correctly: the worktree's .git file points back to the bare repo's worktree metadata, so refs resolve from the bare repo's ref store
Shallow clones (.git/shallow) silently truncate git rev-list; unshallowing with git fetch --unshallow is needed before any commit-history-dependent operations
Post-render DOM highlighting (TreeWalker on text nodes) is more robust than parser-level highlighting because it operates on the final rendered output and handles cross-node matches

friction: pushSchema bug required 4+ debugging iterations before the root cause (comment filter on SQL chunks) was found
macOS com.apple.provenance xattr causing SQLite disk I/O error was unexpected and hard to diagnose
gitlog npm package silently discarded failures from maxBuffer overflow, causing 0 OID mappings with no error surfaced
The 'session not updating' issue was incorrectly attributed to Gossamer code multiple times before discovering the real cause was missing Entire hooks in cosmos-graphql's .claude/settings.json
Per-message author attribution required 3 separate attempts: (1) git blame on jsonl file (wrong approach), (2) shadow branch tip author (incomplete), (3) checkpoint commit author (correct)
Client-side sort in ActiveSessions.tsx was overriding the server's carefully computed tiered sort order, requiring a separate 'default' sort key to preserve server ordering

Copy button on hover caused text layout shift in multiple components (EventItem, ToolUseItem, SessionDetail ClaudeTurnCard) — required fixing opacity-vs-conditional-mount in all three places independently
open_items: SessionDetailPanel.ts does not inject window.__GOSSAMER_REPO_PATH__, so localPath is never sent on API calls from the session detail view — cross-repo DB resolution relies on the probe fallback

Multi-window server spawn conflict: when 3 VS Code windows activate simultaneously, EADDRINUSE losers spin on 'Starting gossamer server' forever — the installed extension's bundled-server doesn't have the probe-before-spawn fix
Cross-node search term highlighting (matches spanning multiple inline nodes like bold + plain text) is not implemented; documented as a known gap in MarkdownView

Author backfill for LogEvents that have no sessionLinkId and whose originating shadow branch has been pruned (e.g. mux-thumbnail-refactor) cannot be resolved via checkpoint commits and falls back to 'You'
Entire CLI not writing to full.jsonl in cosmos-graphql because hooks were missing from .claude/settings.json — fixed the hooks file but underlying cause (hooks getting removed) is not addressed
Checkpoint remote feature (bare repo cloning from entireio separate checkpoint repo) was implemented but not tested end-to-end against a real checkpoint remote URL

Live session indexer (indexLiveSessions from .entire/metadata/) was added but may have been reverted along with other changes — the transcript references a revert and suggests the feature may not be in the final build
